nodes.name: Time Complexity
nodes.definition:
It is a type of analysis to determine the approximate time required by an
algorithm to complete a given task when the number of inputs increases.
nodes.last_question_asked: If you were tasked with designing an algorithm that must handle a dataset that doubles in size every hour, how would the difference between linear and quadratic time complexity impact the long-term feasibility of your system's performance?

nodes.name: Big O notation
nodes.definition:
It describes worst case scenario of an algorithm to complete a task when
input size increases.
nodes.last_question_asked: If you were tasked with designing an algorithm to search for a specific value in a dataset, how would the Big O complexity of that search change if you transitioned from an unsorted list to a sorted one, and what does this imply about the relationship between data organization and algorithm efficiency?
